private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Vector Defense, a Series A-stage defense and security company that has raised $61M to date, sits within a competitive frontier-tech sector comprising 8 private peers and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The private peer set includes companies at Series B and later stages, with valuations spanning $125M to $3.0B.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for post-money value, applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $122M. Peer-Median analysis across the 8 same-sector comparables—including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B)—produces a median benchmark of $1.0B, with a range of $125M to $3.0B.

Triangulation across these two methods supports a valuation range of $104M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $561M. The wide range reflects meaningful uncertainty: the stage-progression method is constrained by limited fundraising history, while peer comparables span significant heterogeneity in maturity and market positioning. Key uncertainties include Vector Defense's revenue traction, path to profitability, and whether earlier-stage comparables more accurately reflect current position than mature Series B benchmarks. Further due diligence on customer concentration, contract duration, and technical defensibility would narrow this range.
